          \"pod_count\": len(pods),\n284\t                    \"healthy_count\": sum(1 for p in pods if p[\"phase\"] == \"Running\"),\n285\t                }\n286\t\n287\t        except FetchOrchestrator:\n820\t    \"\"\"\n821\t    Orchestrates concurrent fetch execution with streaming support.\n822\t\n823\t    This is a convenience wrapper around FetchStrand that provides the same interface.\n824\t    The actual fetch execution is handled by the FetchStrand instance.\n825\t\n826\t    Features:\n827\t    - Concurrent execution of all fetch sources\n828\t    - Streaming callbacks as sources complete\n829\t    - Per-source timeout enforcement\n830\t    - Coverage tracking (succeeded, timed_out, failed, skipped)\n831\t    - Caveat generation for failed sources\n832\t    \"\"\"\n833\t\n834\t    def __init__(self, fetch_strand: Optional[FetchStrand] = None):\n835\t        \"\"\"\n836\t        Initialize the orchestrator.\n837\t\n838\t        Args:\n839\t            fetch_strand: FetchStrand instance (uses global if not provided)\n840\t        \"\"\"\n841\t        if fetch_strand is None:\n842\t            self._strand = FetchStrand()\n843\t        else:\n844\t            self._strand = fetch_strand\n845\t\n846\t    async def execute_fetch(\n847\t        self,\n848\t        request: FetchRequest,\n849\t        stream_callback: Optional[StreamCallback] = None,\n850\t    ) -> FetchResult:\n851\t        \"\"\"\n852\t        Execute a fetch request with concurrent source execution.\n853\t\n854\t        Args:\n855\t            request: The fetch request with intent type and context\n856\t            stream_callback: Optional callback for incremental results\n857\t\n858\t        Returns:\n859\t            FetchResult with all source results and coverage info\n860\t        \"\"\"\n861\t        logger.info(\n862\t            f\"Starting fetch for intent {request.intent_id} \"\n863\t            f\"(type={request.intent_type.value})\"\n864\t        )\n865\t\n866\t        # Delegate to FetchStrand which handles:\n867\t        # - Concurrent execution\n868\t        # - Per-source timeouts\n869\t        # - Streaming callbacks\n870\t        # - Coverage tracking\n871\t        result = await self._strand.fetch(\n872\t            request=request,\n873\t            on_partial_result=stream_callback,\n874\t        )\n875\t\n876\t        logger.info(\n877\t            f\"Fetch complete for intent {request.intent_id}: \"\n878\t            f\"{result.coverage.success_rate:.1%} success, \"\n879\t            f\"{result.total_duration_ms}ms\"\n880\t        )\n881\t\n882\t        return result\n883\t\n884\t\n885\t# Global orchestrator instance\n886\t_orchestrator: Optional[FetchOrchestrator] = None\n887\t\n888\t\n889\tdef get_orchestrator() -> FetchOrchestrator:\n890\t    \"\"\"Get or create the global orchestrator instance.\"\"\"\n891\t    global _orchestrator\n892\t    if _orchestrator is orchestrator: coordinates concurrent fetch execution with streaming support.\n\nRuns all fetch sources concurrently, provides incremental results via callbacks,\ntracks coverage, and surfaces caveats for failed sources.\n\nThis is the canonical fetch implementation, consolidating the command matrix,\nconcurrent execution, and coverage tracking into a single module.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport asyncio\nimport time\nfrom collections import defaultdict\nfrom logging import getLogger\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Any, AsyncIterator, Callable, Optional\n\nimport httpx\n\nfrom get_required_sources,\n    SourceResult,\n)\n\n\nlogger = getLogger(__name__)\n\n# ArgoCD read-only proxy endpoint\nARGOCD_RO_PROXY = \"https://argocd-ro-ardenone-manager-ts.ardenone.com:8444\"\n\n# Type alias for the streaming callback\n# Called when a source completes: (source, result) -> None\nStreamCallback = Callable[[FetchSource, SourceResult], None]\n\n\nclass FetchStrand:\n    \"\"\"\n    Fetch strand: concurrent data fetcher with streaming support.\n\n    Executes all fetch commands for an intent concurrently, streams partial\n    results as they arrive, and provides comprehensive coverage tracking.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self):\n        self._source_executors = {\n            FetchSource.KUBECTL_PODS: self._fetch_kubectl_pods,\n            FetchSource.KUBECTL_DEPLOYMENTS: self._fetch_kubectl_deployments,\n            FetchSource.KUBECTL_WORKFLOWS: self._fetch_kubectl_workflows,\n            FetchSource.ARGOCD_APP: self._fetch_argocd_app,\n            FetchSource.GIT_LOG: self._fetch_git_log,\n            FetchSource.GIT_STATUS: self._fetch_git_status,\n            FetchSource.BEAD_LIST: self._fetch_bead_list,\n            FetchSource.BEAD_DETAILS: self._fetch_bead_details,\n            FetchSource.CI_STATUS: self._fetch_ci_status,\n            FetchSource.COMPONENTS: self._fetch_components,\n            FetchSource.LOGS: self._fetch_logs,\n            FetchSource.EVENTS: self._fetch_events,\n            FetchSource.SESSION_STATE: self._fetch_session_state,\n            FetchSource.TOPIC_CONTEXT: Optional[Callable[[FetchSource, SourceResult], None]] = None,\n    ) -> FetchResult:\n        \"\"\"\n        Execute fetch request with optional streaming callback.\n\n        Args:\n            request: The fetch request to execute\n            on_partial_result: Optional callback for streaming partial results\n\n        Returns:\n            FetchResult with coverage information\n        \"\"\"\n        start_time = time.time()\n\n        # Get fetch commands for this intent type\n        command_specs = get_fetch_commands(request.intent_type)\n        required_sources = get_required_sources(request.intent_type)\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Fetching {len(command_specs)} sources for intent {request.intent_id} \"\n            f\"(type: {request.intent_type.value})\"\n        )\n\n        # Create tasks for concurrent execution\n        tasks = []\n        for spec in command_specs:\n            task = asyncio.create_task(\n                self._execute_source(spec, request.context),\n                name=f\"fetch_{spec.source.value}_{request.intent_id[:8]}\",\n            )\n            tasks.append((spec.source, spec.required, spec.timeout_seconds, task))\n\n        # Wait for all tasks to complete (with per-task timeout)\n        sources = {}\n        succeeded = []\n        timed_out = []\n        failed = []\n        skipped = []\n        caveats = []\n\n        for source, required, timeout, task in tasks:\n            try:\n                result = await asyncio.wait_for(task, timeout=timeout)\n\n                if on_partial_result:\n                    # Stream partial result as it arrives\n                    on_partial_result(source, result)\n\n                sources[source] = result\n\n                if result.status == \"success\":\n                    succeeded.append(source)\n                elif result.status == \"timeout\":\n                    timed_out.append(source)\n                    caveats.append(f\"{source.value} timed out\")\n                elif result.status == \"error\":\n                    failed.append(source)\n                    if required:\n                        caveats.append(f\"Required source {source.value} failed: {result.error}\")\n                    else:\n                        caveats.append(f\"Optional source {source.value} failed: {result.error}\")\n\n            except asyncio.TimeoutError:\n                logger.warning(f\"Source {source.value} timed out after {timeout}s\")\n                sources[source] = SourceResult(\n                    source=source,\n                    status=\"timeout\",\n                    data={},\n                    error=f\"Timed out after {timeout}s\",\n                    duration_ms=int(timeout * 1000),\n                )\n                timed_out.append(source)\n                if required:\n                    caveats.append(f\"Required source {source.value} timed out\")\n\n            except Exception as e:\n                logger.error(f\"Error fetching {source.value}: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n                sources[source] = SourceResult(\n                    source=source,\n                    status=\"error\",\n                    data={},\n                    error=str(e),\n                    duration_ms=0,\n                )\n                failed.append(source)\n                if required:\n                    caveats.append(f\"Required source {source.value} crashed: {e}\")\n\n        # Build coverage report\n        coverage = FetchCoverage(\n            total_sources=len(command_specs),\n            succeeded=succeeded,\n            timed_out=timed_out,\n            failed=failed,\n            skipped=skipped,\n        )\n\n        total_duration_ms = int((time.time() - start_time) * 1000)\n\n        result = FetchResult(\n            intent_id=request.intent_id,\n            intent_type=request.intent_type,\n            sources=sources,\n            coverage=coverage,\n            total_duration_ms=total_duration_ms,\n            caveats=caveats if caveats else None,\n        )\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Fetch complete for intent {request.intent_id}: \"\n            f\"{len(succeeded)}/{coverage.total_sources} succeeded, \"\n            f\"{len(timed_out)} timed out, {len(failed)} failed\"\n        )\n\n        return result\n\n    async def _execute_source(\n        self,\n        spec,\n        context: FetchContext,\n    ) -> SourceResult:\n        \"\"\"Execute a single fetch source.\"\"\"\n        executor = self._source_executors.get(spec.source)\n        if not executor:\n            return SourceResult(\n                source=spec.source,\n                status=\"error\",\n                data={},\n                error=f\"No executor for source {spec.source.value}\",\n                duration_ms=0,\n            )\n\n        start = time.time()\n        try:\n            data = await executor(context)\n            duration_ms = int((time.time() - start) * 1000)\n\n            return SourceResult(\n                source=spec.source,\n 1000)\n            return SourceResult(\n                source=spec.source,\n                status=\"timeout\",\n                data={},\n                error=f\"Timed out after {spec.timeout_seconds}s\",\n                duration_ms=duration_ms,\n            )\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            duration_ms = int((time.time() - start) * 1000)\n            return SourceResult(\n                source=spec.source,\n                status=\"error\",\n                data={},\n                error=str(e),\n                duration_ms=duration_ms,\n            )\n\n    # Source executors\n\n    async def _fetch_kubectl_pods(self, context: FetchContext) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Fetch pod status from kubernetes.\"\"\"\n        proxy = context.proxy\n        namespace = context.namespace\n\n        if not namespace:\n            # Try to derive from project_slug\n            if context.project_slug:\n                namespace = context.project_slug.replace(\"-\", \"\")\n            else:\n                return {\"error\": \"No namespace specified\"}\n\n        try:\n            async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=10.0) as          phase = pod.get(\"status\", {}).get(\"phase\", \"Unknown\")\n                    ready = self._get_pod_ready(pod)\n                    restarts = sum(\n FetchContext) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Fetch git log for a project (local or remote via SSH).\"\"\"\n        repo_path = context.repo_path\n        if not repo_path:\n            return {\"error\": \"No repo path resolved\"}\n\n        if not context.ssh_target and not Path(repo_path).exists():\n            return {\"error\": f\"Repo path not found: {repo_path}\"}\n\n        try:\n            # Fetch commits with author and date\n            proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n                *self._make_cmd(context, \"git\", \"-C\", repo_path, \"log\", \"-10\",\n                                \"--pretty=format:%h|%s|%an|%ar\"),\n                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n            )\n\n            stdout, stderr = await proc.communicate()\n\n            if proc.returncode != 0:\n                return {\"error\": stderr.decode()}\n\n            commits = []\n            for line in stdout.decode().strip().split(\"\\n\"):\n                if line:\n                    parts = line.split(\"|\", 3)\n                    if len(parts) == 4:\n                        commits.append({\n                            \"hash\": parts[0],\n                            \"message\": parts[1],\n                            \"author\": parts[2],\n                            \"date\": parts[3],\n                        })\n\n            # Get current branch\n            branch_proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n                *self._make_cmd(context, \"git\", \"-C\", repo_path, \"branch\", \"--show-current\"),\n                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n            )\n            branch_stdout, _ = await branch_proc.communicate()\n            branch = branch_stdout.decode().strip()\n\n            return {\n                \"repo\": repo_path,\n                \"branch\": branch,\n                \"commits\": commits,\n                \"count\": len(commits),\n            }\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            return {\"error\": str(e), \"commits\": []}\n\n    async def _fetch_git_status(self, context: FetchContext) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Fetch git status for a project (local or remote via SSH).\"\"\"\n        repo_path = context.repo_path\n        if not repo_path:\n            return {\"error\": \"No repo path resolved\"}\n        if not context.ssh_target and not Path(repo_path).exists():\n            return {\"error\": f\"Repo path not found: {repo_path}\"}\n\n        try:\n            # Get status output\n            proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n                \"git\",\n                \"-C\", repo_path,\n                \"status\", \"--short\",\n                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n            )\n\n            stdout, stderr = await proc.communicate()\n\n            if proc.returncode != 0:\n                return {\"error\": stderr.decode()}\n\n            # Parse status output - format: XY filename\n            changed_files = []\n            for line in stdout.decode().strip().split(\"\\n\"):\n                if line and len(line) >= 3:\n                    status_code = line[:2]\n                    filename = line[3:]\n                    changed_files.append({\n                        \"status\": status_code,\n                        \"file\": filename,\n                    })\n\n            # Get current branch\n            branch_proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n                \"git\",\n                \"-C\", repo_path,\n                \"branch\", \"--show-current\",\n                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n            )\n            branch_stdout, _ = await branch_proc.communicate()\n            branch = branch_stdout.decode().strip()\n\n            # Get last commit\n            log_proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n                \"git\",\n                \"-C\", repo_path,\n                \"log\", \"-1\", \"--oneline\",\n                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n            )\n            log_stdout, _ = await log_proc.communicate()\n            last_commit = log_stdout.decode().strip()\n\n            return {\n                \"repo\": repo_path,\n                \"branch\": branch,\n                \"last_commit\": last_commit,\n                \"changed_files\": changed_files,\n                \"count\": len(changed_files),\n                \"has_changes\": len(changed_files) > 0,\n            }\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            return {\"error\": str(e), \"changed_files\": []}\n\n    async def _fetch_bead_list(self, context: FetchContext) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Fetch bead    )\n\n            stdout, stderr = await proc.communicate()\n\n            if proc.returncode == 0:\n                import json as _json\n                try:\n                    beads = patterns.\"\"\"\n    HOW_LONG = \"how_long\"  # \"how long until...\", \"when will...\"\n    WHY = \"why\"  # \"why did...\", \"why is...\"\n    WHAT_CHANGED = \"what_changed\"  # \"what changed...\", \"what's different...\"\n    IS_READY = \"is_ready\"  # \"is it ready...\", \"is it done...\"\n    MORE_DETAIL = \"more_detail\"  # \"tell me more...\", \"more detail...\"\n    STATUS_CHECK = \"status_check\"  # \"what's the status...\", \"how's it going...\"\n    ERROR_DETAILS = \"error_details\"  # \"what's the error...\", \"why failed...\"\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass PrefetchPrediction:\n    \"\"\"A prediction of what data might be needed next.\"\"\"\n    pattern: FollowUpPattern\n    topic_id: str\n    project_slugs: list[str]\n    intent_type: str\n    confidence: float\n    prefetch_data: dict[str, Any]\n    created_at: int\n    expires_at: int\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass PrefetchCache:\n    \"\"\"Cache for pre-fetched data.\"\"\"\n    key: str  # topic_id:pattern\n    data: dict[str, Any]\n    created_at: int\n    expires_at: int\n    hit_count: int = 0\n    last_hit: int = 0\n\n    def is_valid(self) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Check if cache entry is still valid.\"\"\"\n        return int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()) < self.expires_at\n\n    def record_hit(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Record a cache hit.\"\"\"\n        self.hit_count += 1\n        self.last_hit = int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp())\n\n\nclass SpeculativePrefetcher:\n    \"\"\"\n    Speculative pre-fetcher for common follow-up patterns.\n\n    Monitors conversation patterns and pre-fetches data that's likely\n    to be requested next, making follow-up responses faster.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, ttl_seconds: int = 300):\n        self.ttl_seconds = ttl_seconds\n        self._cache: dict[str, PrefetchCache] = {}\n        self._recent_utterances: dict[str, list[dict]] = {}  # session_id -> utterances\n        self._fetch_callbacks: dict[FollowUpPattern, Callable] = {}\n        self._fetch_executor = get_fetch_executor()\n\n        # Register default fetch callbacks\n        self._register_default_callbacks()\n\n    def _register_default_callbacks(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Register default fetch callbacks for each pattern.\"\"\"\n        async def fetch_how_long(topic_id: str, project_slugs: list[str]) -> dict:\n            \"\"\"Pre-fetch: CI/build status timestamps, deployment times.\"\"\"\n            result = {}\n            for project_slug in project_slugs:\n                # Fetch CI status for timing info\n                ci_cmd = FetchCommand(\n                    fetch_type=FetchType.CI_STATUS,\n                    project_slug=project_slug,\n                    args=[],\n                    timeout=30,\n                )\n                ci_result = await self._fetch_executor.execute(ci_cmd)\n                if ci_result.success:\n                    result[project_slug] = {\"ci_status\": ci_result.data}\n\n                # Fetch deployment status\n                deploy_cmd = FetchCommand(\n                    fetch_type=FetchType.DEPLOYMENT_STATUS,\n                    project_slug=project_slug,\n                    args=[],\n                    timeout=30,\n                )\n                deploy_result = await self._fetch_executor.execute(deploy_cmd)\n                if deploy_result.success:\n                    if project_slug not in result:\n                        result[project_slug] = {}\n                    result[project_slug][\"deployment\"] = deploy_result.data\n            return result\n\n        async def fetch_why(topic_id: str, project_slugs: list[str]) -> dict:\n            \"\"\"Pre-fetch: logs, error messages, recent events.\"\"\"\n            # For now, return recent status that might explain why something happened\n            result = {}\n            for project_slug in project_slugs:\n                status_cmd = FetchCommand(\n                    fetch_type=FetchType.KUBECTL_STATUS,\n                    project_slug=project_slug,\n                    args=[],\n                    timeout=30,\n                )\n                status_result = await self._fetch_executor.execute(status_cmd)\n                if status_result.success:\n                    result[project_slug] = {\n                        \"pods\": status_result.data.get(\"pods\", []),\n                        \"recent_events\": status_result.data.get(\"events\", []),\n                    }\n            return result\n\n        async def fetch_what_changed(topic_id: str, project_slugs: list[str]) -> dict:\n            \"\"\"Pre-fetch: git diff, recent commits, history.\"\"\"\n            result = {}\n            for project_slug in project_slugs:\n                git_cmd = FetchCommand(\n                    fetch_type=FetchType.GIT_LOG,\n                    project_slug=project_slug,\n                    args=[],\n                    timeout=30,\n                )\n                git_result = await self._fetch_executor.execute(git_cmd)\n                if git_result.success:\n                    result[project_slug] = git_result.data\n            return result\n\n        async def fetch_is_ready(topic_id: str, project_slugs: list[str]) -> dict:\n            \"\"\"Pre-fetch: health status, ready condition, completion status.\"\"\"\n            result = {}\n            for project_slug in project_slugs:\n                status_cmd = FetchCommand(\n                    fetch_type=FetchType.KUBECTL_STATUS,\n                    project_slug=project_slug,\n                    args=[],\n                    timeout=30,\n                )\n                status_result = await self._fetch_executor.execute(status_cmd)\n                if status_result.success:\n                    pods = status_result.data.get(\"pods\", [])\n                    ready_count = sum(1 for p in pods if p.get(\"phase\") == \"Running\")\n                    result[project_slug] = {\n                        \"health\": \"healthy\" if ready_count == len(pods) and len(pods) > 0 else \"degraded\",\n                        \"ready\": ready_count == len(pods) and len(pods) > 0,\n                        \"completion\": f\"{ready_count}/{len(pods)}\" if pods else \"0/0\",\n                    }\n            return result\n\n        async def fetch_more_detail(topic_id: str, project_slugs: list[str]) -> dict:\n            \"\"\"Pre-fetch: extended metrics, detailed status.\"\"\"\n            result = {}\n            for project_slug in project_slugs:\n                # Fetch comprehensive status\n                status_cmd = FetchCommand(\n                    fetch_type=FetchType.KUBECTL_STATUS,\n                    project_slug=project_slug,\n                    args=[],\n                    timeout=30,\n                )\n                status_result = await self._fetch_executor.execute(status_cmd)\n                if status_result.success:\n                    result[project_slug] = status_result.data\n\n                # Fetch ArgoCD status if available\n                argocd_cmd = FetchCommand(\n                    fetch_type=FetchType.ARGOCD_STATUS,\n                    project_slug=project_slug,\n                    args=[],\n                    timeout=30,\n                )\n                argocd_result = await self._fetch_executor.execute(argocd_cmd)\n                if argocd_result.success:\n                    if project_slug not in result:\n                        result[project_slug] = {}\n                    result[project_slug][\"argocd\"] = argocd_result.data\n            return result\n\n        async def fetch_status_check(topic_id: str, project_slugs: list[str]) -> dict:\n            \"\"\"Pre-fetch: current status, phase, health.\"\"\"\n            result = {}\n            for project_slug in project_slugs:\n                status_cmd = FetchCommand(\n                    fetch_type=FetchType.KUBECTL_STATUS,\n                    project_slug=project_slug,\n                    args=[],\n                    timeout=30,\n                )\n                status_result = await self._fetch_executor.execute(status_cmd)\n                if status_result.success:\n                    pods = status_result.data.get(\"pods\", [])\n                    result[project_slug] = {\n                        \"status\": \"running\" if any(p.get(\"phase\") == \"Running\" for p in pods) else \"not_running\",\n                        \"phase\": next((p.get(\"phase\") for p in pods if p.get(\"phase\") != \"Succeeded\"), \"Unknown\"),\n                        \"health\": \"healthy\" if all(p.get(\"phase\") in (\"Running\", \"Succeeded\") for p in pods) else \"degraded\",\n                    }\n            return result\n\n        async def fetch_error_details(topic_id: str, project_slugs: list[str]) -> dict:\n            \"\"\"Pre-fetch: error logs, failure reasons, stack traces.\"\"\"\n            result = {}\n            for project_slug in project_slugs:\n                # Fetch CI status for error details\n                ci_cmd = FetchCommand(\n                    fetch_type=FetchType.CI_STATUS,\n                    project_slug=project_slug,\n                    args=[],\n                    timeout=30,\n                )\n                ci_result = await self._fetch_executor.execute(ci_cmd)\n                if ci_result.success:\n                    workflows = ci_result.data.get(\"workflows\", [])\n                    failed_workflows = [w for w in workflows if w.get(\"phase\") == \"Failed\"]\n                    result[project_slug] = {\n                        \"error_logs\": [w.get(\"message\") for w in failed_workflows],\n                        \"failure_reason\": failed_workflows[0].get(\"message\") if failed_workflows else None,\n                        \"stack_trace\": None,  # Would need full workflow fetch\n                    }\n            return result\n\n        self._fetch_callbacks = {\n            FollowUpPattern.HOW_LONG: fetch_how_long,\n            FollowUpPattern.WHY: fetch_why,\n            FollowUpPattern.WHAT_CHANGED: fetch_what_changed,\n            FollowUpPattern.IS_READY: fetch_is_ready,\n            FollowUpPattern.MORE_DETAIL: fetch_more_detail,\n            FollowUpPattern.STATUS_CHECK: fetch_status_check,\n            FollowUpPattern.ERROR_DETAILS: fetch_error_details,\n        }\n\n    def register_fetch_callback(\n        self,\n        pattern: FollowUpPattern,\n        callback: Callable,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Register a custom fetch callback for a pattern.\"\"\"\n        self._fetch_callbacks[pattern] = callback\n\n    async def analyze_utterance(\n        self,\n        session_id: str,\n        utterance: str,\n        topic_id: str,\n        project_slugs: list[str],\n        intent_type: str,\n    ) -> list[PrefetchPrediction]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Analyze an utterance and generate prefetch predictions.\n\n        Returns a list of predictions with confidence scores.\n        \"\"\"\n        predictions = []\n\n        # Detect patterns in utterance\n        utterance_lower = utterance.lower()\n\n        # HOW_LONG pattern: \"how long\", \"when\", \"time remaining\", \"ETA\"\n        if re.search(r\"how long|when will|time remaining|eta|estimated\", utterance_lower):\n            predictions.append(PrefetchPrediction(\n                pattern=FollowUpPattern.HOW_LONG,\n                topic_id=topic_id,\n                project_slugs=project_slugs,\n                intent_type=intent_type,\n                confidence=0.8,\n                prefetch_data={},\n                created_at=int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n                expires_at=int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()) + self.ttl_seconds,\n            ))\n\n        # WHY pattern: \"why\", \"how come\", \"reason\"\n        if re.search(r\"why|how come|reason for|what caused\", utterance_lower):\n            predictions.append(PrefetchPrediction(\n                pattern=FollowUpPattern.WHY,\n                topic_id=topic_id,\n                project_slugs=project_slugs,\n                intent_type=intent_type,\n                confidence=0.7,\n                prefetch_data={},\n                created_at=int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n                expires_at=int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()) + self.ttl_seconds,\n            ))\n\n        # WHAT_CHANGED pattern: \"what changed\", \"what's different\", \"since\"\n        if re.search(r\"what changed|what's different|what's new|since|from before\", utterance_lower):\n            predictions.append(PrefetchPrediction(\n                pattern=FollowUpPattern.WHAT_CHANGED,\n                topic_id=topic_id,\n                project_slugs=project_slugs,\n                intent_type=intent_type,\n                confidence=0.75,\n                prefetch_data={},\n                created_at=int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n                expires_at=int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()) + self.ttl_seconds,\n            ))\n\n        # IS_READY pattern: \"is it ready\", \"is it done\", \"finished\", \"complete\"\n        if re.search(r\"is it ready|is it done|finished|complete|ready yet\", utterance_lower):\n            predictions.append(PrefetchPrediction(\n                pattern=FollowUpPattern.IS_READY,\n                topic_id=topic_id,\n                project_slugs=project_slugs,\n                intent_type=intent_type,\n                confidence=0.85,\n                prefetch_data={},\n                created_at=int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n                expires_at=int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()) + self.ttl_seconds,\n            ))\n\n        # Store utterance for context\n        self._store_utterance(session_id, utterance, topic_id, intent_type)\n\n        return predictions\n\n    def _store_utterance(\n        self,\n        session_id: str,\n        utterance: str,\n        topic_id: str,\n        intent_type: str,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Store utterance for pattern analysis.\"\"\"\n        if session_id not in self._recent_utterances:\n            self._recent_utterances[session_id] = []\n\n        self._recent_utterances[session_id].append({\n            \"utterance\": utterance,\n            \"topic_id\": topic_id,\n            \"intent_type\": intent_type,\n            \"timestamp\": int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n        })\n\n        # Keep only last 20 utterances\n        if len(self._recent_utterances[session_id]) > 20:\n            self._recent_utterances[session_id] = self._recent_utterances[session_id][-20:]\n\n    async def prefetch_for_predictions(\n        self,\n        predictions: list[PrefetchPrediction],\n    ) -> dict[str, PrefetchCache]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Execute prefetch for a list of predictions.\n\n        Returns a dict of cache entries keyed by pattern.\n        \"\"\"\n        cache_entries = {}\n\n        for prediction in predictions:\n            # Skip low confidence predictions\n            if prediction.confidence < 0.5:\n                continue\n\n            # Check if we already have valid cache\n            cache_key = f\"{prediction.topic_id}:{prediction.pattern.value}\"\n            if cache_key in self._cache and self._cache[cache_key].is_valid():\n                logger.debug(f\"Cache hit for {cache_key}\")\n                cache_entries[cache_key] = self._cache[cache_key]\n                continue\n\n            # Fetch data\n            callback = self._fetch_callbacks.get(prediction.pattern)\n            if not callback:\n                logger.warning(f\"No fetch callback for pattern {prediction.pattern}\")\n                continue\n\n            try:\n                data = await callback(\n                    prediction.topic_id,\n                    prediction.project_slugs,\n                )\n\n                # Create cache entry\n                cache_entry = PrefetchCache(\n                    key=cache_key,\n                    data=data,\n                    created_at=int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n                    expires_at=prediction.expires_at,\n                )\n\n                self._cache[cache_key] = cache_entry\n                cache_entries[cache_key] = cache_entry\n\n                logger.info(f\"Prefetched data for {cache_key}\")\n\n            except Exception as e:\n                logger.error(f\"Prefetch error for {prediction.pattern}: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n\n        return cache_entries\n\n    def get_prefetch_data(\n        self,\n        topic_id: str,\n        pattern: FollowUpPattern,\n    ) -> Optional[dict]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Get pre-fetched data for a topic and pattern.\n\n        Returns None if no valid cache exists.\n        \"\"\"\n        cache_key = f\"{topic_id}:{pattern.value}\"\n\n        if cache_key not in self._cache:\n            return None\n\n        cache_entry = self._cache[cache_key]\n\n        if not cache_entry.is_valid():\n            # Cache expired, remove it\n            del self._cache[cache_key]\n            return None\n\n        # Record cache hit\n        cache_entry.record_hit()\n\n        return cache_entry.data\n\n    def get_all_prefetch_data(self, topic_id: str) -> dict[str, Any]:\n        \"\"\"Get all pre-fetched data for a topic.\"\"\"\n        result = {}\n        prefix = f\"{topic_id}:\"\n\n        for key, cache_entry in self._cache.items():\n            if key.startswith(prefix) and cache_entry.is_valid():\n                pattern = key[len(prefix):]\n                result[pattern] = {\n                    \"data\": cache_entry.data,\n                    \"hit_count\": cache_entry.hit_count,\n                    \"expires_at\": cache_entry.expires_at,\n                }\n\n        return result\n\n    async def cleanup_expired(self) -> int:\n        \"\"\"Clean up expired cache entries. Returns count of cleaned entries.\"\"\"\n        now = int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp())\n        expired_keys = [\n            key for key, cache in self._cache.items()\n            if cache.expires_at < now\n        ]\n\n        for key in expired_keys:\n            del self._cache[key]\n\n        if expired_keys:\n            logger.info(f\"Cleaned up {len(expired_keys)} expired prefetch entries\")\n\n        return len(expired_keys)\n\n    def get_stats(self) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Get prefetch statistics.\"\"\"\n        total_hits = sum(cache.hit_count for cache in self._cache.values())\n\n        return {\n            \"cache_size\": len(self._cache),\n            \"total_hits\": total_hits,\n            \"valid_entries\": sum(1 for cache in self._cache.values() if cache.is_valid()),\n            \"expired_entries\": sum(1 for cache in self._cache.values() if not cache.is_valid()),\n        }\n\n    def clear_session(self, session_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Clear utterance history for a session.\"\"\"\n        if session_id in self._recent_utterances:\n            del self._recent_utterances[session_id]\n\n\n# Global prefetcher instance\n_prefetcher: Optional[SpeculativePrefetcher] = None\n\n\ndef get_prefetcher(ttl_seconds: int = 300) -> SpeculativePrefetcher:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the global speculative prefetcher instance.\"\"\"\n    global _prefetcher\n    if _prefetcher is None:\n        _prefetcher = SpeculativePrefetcher(ttl_seconds=ttl_seconds)\n    return _prefetcher\n","numLines":497,"startLine":1,"totalLines":497}}}

A compound utterance touching five projects takes as long as the slowest single agent, not their sum.\n14\t\n15\t3. **Fetch strand** — each agent runs deterministic commands against live data sources (kubectl, git, local repos, SSH targets) to gather raw context for its thread.\n16\t\n17\t4. **Synthesize strand** — one LLM call per thread converts the raw data into a 2–3 sentence structured result.\n18\t\n19\t5. **Canvas delivery** — results stream to the browser via SSE and render as HTML cards, one per topic, updated in place as agents resolve.\n20\t\n21\t```\n22\t  Voice / Text Input\n23\t  \"has the pipeline caught up? also what's the ibkr mcp status?\"\n24\t                        │\n25\t                        ▼\n26\t             ┌──────────────────┐\n27\t             │   Intent Router  │  ← one LLM call, splits utterance\n28\t             └──────────────────┘\n29\t                        │\n30\t          ┌─────────────┴──────────────┐\n31\t          ▼                            ▼\n32\t   [agent: pipeline]           [agent: ibkr-mcp]\n33\t   Fetch → Synthesize           Fetch → Synthesize\n34\t          │                            │\n35\t          └──────────────┬─────────────┘\n36\t                         ▼\n37\t              ┌─────────────────────────┐\n38\t              │  Canvas (SSE)           │\n39\t              │  ┌─────────┐ ┌───────┐  │\n40\t              │  │pipeline │ │ibkr   │  │\n41\t              │  │ card    │ │ card  │  │\n42\t              │  └─────────┘ └───────┘  │\n43\t              └─────────────────────────┘\n44\t```\n45\t\n46\t**Task-profile intents** skip fetch/synthesize. Instead, a work item is created and picked up asynchronously by an async worker fleet. When the work completes, the result is pushed to the canvas via the bead watcher daemon.\n47\t\n48\t---\n49\t\n50\t## Features\n51\t\n52\t### Input methods\n53\t- **Text**: browser canvas at `http://localhost:8000/` or CLI: `adc dispatch \"...\"`\n54\t- **Voice**: OpenAI Realtime API (WebRTC) — speak directly into the browser\n55\t\n56\t### Live canvas\n57\tSingle-page dark-themed UI (`src/canvas/index.html`). Results stream in via Server-Sent Events. Each distinct topic (project + intent) gets its own card, updated in place as results arrive. No page reload needed.\n58\t\n59\t### Voice narration\n60\tResults are narrated at urgency-tiered conversational pauses:\n61\t- `critical` — interrupt immediately\n62\t- `high` — next natural pause\n63\t- `normal` — batched delivery\n64\t- `low` — only when idle\n65\t\n66\t### Self-modification and hot-reload\n67\tAll behavior-defining artifacts are plain files, re-read on every invocation:\n68\t- `prompts/router.md`, `prompts/synthesize.md`, `prompts/voice.md`, `prompts/urgency.md`\n69\t- `config/registry.yaml` — project and alias definitions\n70\t- `config/monitoring.yaml` — ambient monitoring rules\n71\t- `data/components.db` — versioned HTML/CSS card templates (SQLite)\n72\t\n73\tA spoken instruction like \"the deploy card is too cluttered\" triggers the `SelfModificationAgent`, which generates a diff, surfaces it for approval, and hot-reloads the artifact. No redeploy needed.\n74\t\n75\t### Project registry\n76\t`config/registry.yaml` defines explicit project entries with cluster targets, namespaces, aliases, and workflow templates. aide-de-camp also auto-discovers git repos at startup. Refresh via `POST /api/v1/environment/refresh`.\n77\t\n78\t### Session persistence\n79\tSession state lives in a SQLite WAL database (`data/session.db`). Tables: `sessions`, `surfaces`, `utterances`, `intents`, `topics`, `results`. The same session is accessible from the canvas, CLI, or audio mode simultaneously.\n80\t\n81\t---\n82\t\n83\t## Quick Start\n84\t\n85\t```bash\n86\t# Clone and install\n87\tgit clone <repo-url> aide-de-camp\n88\tcd aide-de-camp\n89\t\n90\tpip install -e \".[dev]\"\n91\t# or\n92\tpip install -r requirements.txt\n93\t\n94\t# Start the server\n95\tuvicorn src.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000\n96\t\n97\t# Verify it's running\n98\tcurl -s http://localhost:8000/health\n99\t```\n100\t\n101\tOpen `http://localhost:8000/` in your browser to use the canvas.\n102\t\n103\tFor voice mode, an OpenAI API key with Realtime API access is required (set `OPENAI_API_KEY`).\n104\t\n105\t---\n106\t\n107\t## CLI Usage\n108\t\n109\t```bash\n110\t# Route an utterance and stream results to the canvas\n111\tadc dispatch \"has the options pipeline caught up?\"\n112\t\n113\t# Query a specific topic\n114\tadc ask \"what name candidates did we have for pdftract?\"\n115\t\n116\t# Current session summary\n117\tadc status\n118\t\n119\t# List active topic cards\n120\tadc topics\n121\t\n122\t# Show exception queue\n123\tadc exceptions\n124\t\n125\t# Configure the CLI\n126\tadc config --set-server http://myhost:8000\n127\tadc config --set-session <session-id>\n128\t\n129\t# Help\n130\tadc --help\n131\t```\n132\t\n133\t---\n134\t\n135\t## Configuration\n136\t\n137\t### Environment variables\n138\t\n139\t| Variable | Purpose | Default |\n140\t|----------|---------|---------|\n141\t| `OPENAI_API_KEY` | OpenAI key — required for voice/Realtime API | _(none; voice disabled without it)_ |\n142\t| `ZAI_PROXY_URL` | ZAI proxy endpoint for LLM calls (routing and synthesis) | `https://zai-proxy-mcp-apexalgo-iad-ts.ardenone.com:8444/v1/messages` |\n143\t| `ADC_SERVER_URL` | Server URL used by the `adc` CLI | `http://localhost:8000` |\n144\t| `ADC_TELEGRAM_BRIDGE_URL` | Telegram bridge URL for async task notifications | `https://telegram-proxy-telegram-bridge-ardenone-cluster-ts.ardenone.com:8444` ✓ |\n145\t\n146\tThe LLM backend for intent routing and synthesis is configured via the `ZAI_PROXY_URL` environment variable. All LLM calls route through the ZAI proxy.\n147\t\n148\t### Prompts directory\n149\t\n150\tAll prompts are hot-reloaded on every invocation. Edit these files to tune behavior without restarting the server:\n151\t\n152\t| File | Purpose |\n153\t|------|---------|\n154\t| `prompts/router.md` | Intent classification and thread segmentation |\n155\t| `prompts/synthesize.md` | Result synthesis (raw data → 2–3 sentence summary) |\n156\t| `prompts/voice.md` | Narration style and pacing |\n157\t| `prompts/urgency.md` | Urgency classification rules |\n158\t\n159\t### Project registry\n160\t\n161\t`config/registry.yaml` defines the projects aide-de-camp knows about. Each entry specifies:\n162\t- `name` and `aliases` — how the router maps utterance fragments to projects\n163\t- `cluster` and `namespace` — where to run kubectl-based fetch commands\n164\t- `workflow_templates` — named multi-step workflows (e.g., \"deploy\", \"rollback\")\n165\t\n166\t---\n167\t\n168\t## Architecture\n169\t\n170\t### Key source files\n171\t\n172\t| File | Responsibility |\n173\t|------|---------------|\n174\t| `src/main.py` | FastAPI app; `/dispatch`, `/voice`, `/sse` endpoints |\n175\t| `src/intent/router.py` | LLM classification and parallel thread dispatch |\n176\t| `src/fetch/commands.py` | Fetch command matrix per intent type |\n177\t| `src/fetch/orchestrator.py` | Parallel fetch execution |\n178\t| `src/synthesize/strand.py` | LLM synthesis strand |\n179\t| `src/session/store.py` | SQLite session store (aiosqlite) |\n180\t| `src/sse/broadcaster.py` | SSE connection registry and event routing |\n181\t| `src/canvas/index.html` | Canvas single-page app |\n182\t| `src/realtime/session.py` | OpenAI Realtime API voice session |\n183\t| `src/watcher/daemon.py` | Async work-item watcher — pushes completed results to canvas |\n184\t| `src/agents/self_modification.py` | Self-improvement agent |\n185\t| `src/environment/discovery.py` | Local and remote repo scanner |\n186\t| `config/registry.yaml` | Explicit project registry |\n187\t| `prompts/` | Hot-reload prompt files |\n188\t\n189\t### Runtime breakdown\n190\t\n191\t| Runtime | Used for |\n192\t|---------|---------|\n193\t| Realtime API | Voice model (persistent session, voice I/O) |\n194\t| Configurable LLM backend | Intent router, synthesize strand |\n195\t| Deterministic code | Fetch strand (kubectl/git execution), bead watcher |\n196\t| Async worker fleet | UI-regen agent, self-modification agent, background analysis, task work items |\n197\t\n198\t### Data flow\n199\t\n200\t```\n201\tPOST /dispatch\n202\t  → intent router (LLM classify)\n203\t  → N parallel: fetch strand → synthesize strand\n204\t  → persist: topic + result in session.db\n205\t  → broadcast SSEEvent(\"result_created\", surface_id)\n206\t  → canvas calls GET /api/v1/sessions/{id}/topics\n207\t  → cards rendered / updated in place\n208\t```\n209\t\n210\t---\n211\t\n212\t## Requirements\n213\t\n214\t- Python 3.11+\n215\t- `fastapi`, `uvicorn[standard]`, `httpx`, `websockets`, `pydantic`, `aiosqlite`\n216\t- Dev: `pytest`, `pytest-asyncio`, `ruff`\n217\t\n218\t---\n219\t\n220\t## Versioning\n221\t\n222\tVersion is in `pyproject.toml` only. A compound utterance touching five projects takes as long as the slowest single agent, not their sum.\n\n3. **Fetch strand** — each agent runs deterministic commands against live data sources (kubectl, git, local repos, SSH targets) to gather raw context for its thread.\n\n4. **Synthesize strand** — one LLM call per thread converts the raw data into a 2–3 sentence structured result.\n\n5. **Canvas delivery** — results stream to the browser via SSE and render as HTML cards, one per topic, updated in place as agents resolve.\n\n```\n  Voice / Text Input\n  \"has the pipeline caught up? also what's the ibkr mcp status?\"\n                        │\n                        ▼\n             ┌──────────────────┐\n             │   Intent Router  │  ← one LLM call, splits utterance\n             └──────────────────┘\n                        │\n          ┌─────────────┴──────────────┐\n          ▼                            ▼\n   [agent: pipeline]           [agent: ibkr-mcp]\n   Fetch → Synthesize           Fetch → Synthesize\n          │                            │\n          └──────────────┬─────────────┘\n                         ▼\n              ┌─────────────────────────┐\n              │  Canvas (SSE)           │\n              │  ┌─────────┐ ┌───────┐  │\n              │  │pipeline │ │ibkr   │  │\n              │  │ card    │ │ card  │  │\n              │  └─────────┘ └───────┘  │\n              └─────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n**Task-profile intents** skip fetch/synthesize. Instead, a work item is created and picked up asynchronously by an async worker fleet. When the work completes, the result is pushed to the canvas via the bead watcher daemon.\n\n---\n\n## Features\n\n### Input methods\n- **Text**: browser canvas at `http://localhost:8000/` or CLI: `adc dispatch \"...\"`\n- **Voice**: OpenAI Realtime API (WebRTC) — speak directly into the browser\n\n### Live canvas\nSingle-page dark-themed UI (`src/canvas/index.html`). Results stream in via Server-Sent Events. Each distinct topic (project + intent) gets its own card, updated in place as results arrive. No page reload needed.\n\n### Voice narration\nResults are narrated at urgency-tiered conversational pauses:\n- `critical` — interrupt immediately\n- `high` — next natural pause\n- `normal` — batched delivery\n- `low` — only when idle\n\n### Self-modification and hot-reload\nAll behavior-defining artifacts are plain files, re-read on every invocation:\n- `prompts/router.md`, `prompts/synthesize.md`, `prompts/voice.md`, `prompts/urgency.md`\n- `config/registry.yaml` — project and alias definitions\n- `config/monitoring.yaml` — ambient monitoring rules\n- `data/components.db` — versioned HTML/CSS card templates (SQLite)\n\nA spoken instruction like \"the deploy card is too cluttered\" triggers the `SelfModificationAgent`, which generates a diff, surfaces it for approval, and hot-reloads the artifact. No redeploy needed.\n\n### Project registry\n`config/registry.yaml` defines explicit project entries with cluster targets, namespaces, aliases, and workflow templates. aide-de-camp also auto-discovers git repos at startup. Refresh via `POST /api/v1/environment/refresh`.\n\n### Session persistence\nSession state lives in a SQLite WAL database (`data/session.db`). Tables: `sessions`, `surfaces`, `utterances`, `intents`, `topics`, `results`. The same session is accessible from the canvas, CLI, or audio mode simultaneously.\n\n---\n\n## Quick Start\n\n```bash\n# Clone and install\ngit clone <repo-url> aide-de-camp\ncd aide-de-camp\n\npip install -e \".[dev]\"\n# or\npip install -r requirements.txt\n\n# Start the server\nuvicorn src.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000\n\n# Verify it's running\ncurl -s http://localhost:8000/health\n```\n\nOpen `http://localhost:8000/` in your browser to use the canvas.\n\nFor voice mode, an OpenAI API key with Realtime API access is required (set `OPENAI_API_KEY`).\n\n---\n\n## CLI Usage\n\n```bash\n# Route an utterance and stream results to the canvas\nadc dispatch \"has the options pipeline caught up?\"\n\n# Query a specific topic\nadc ask \"what name candidates did we have for pdftract?\"\n\n# Current session summary\nadc status\n\n# List active topic cards\nadc topics\n\n# Show exception queue\nadc exceptions\n\n# Configure the CLI\nadc config --set-server http://myhost:8000\nadc config --set-session <session-id>\n\n# Help\nadc --help\n```\n\n---\n\n## Configuration\n\n### Environment variables\n\n| Variable | Purpose | Default |\n|----------|---------|---------|\n| `OPENAI_API_KEY` | OpenAI key — required for voice/Realtime API | _(none; voice disabled without it)_ |\n| `ZAI_PROXY_URL` | ZAI proxy endpoint for LLM calls (routing and synthesis) | `https://zai-proxy-mcp-apexalgo-iad-ts.ardenone.com:8444/v1/messages` |\n| `ADC_SERVER_URL` | Server URL used by the `adc` CLI | `http://localhost:8000` |\n| `ADC_TELEGRAM_BRIDGE_URL` | Telegram bridge URL for async task notifications | `https://telegram-proxy-telegram-bridge-ardenone-cluster-ts.ardenone.com:8444` ✓ |\n\nThe LLM backend for intent routing and synthesis is configured via the `ZAI_PROXY_URL` environment variable. All LLM calls route through the ZAI proxy.\n\n### Prompts directory\n\nAll prompts are hot-reloaded on every invocation. Edit these files to tune behavior without restarting the server:\n\n| File | Purpose |\n|------|---------|\n| `prompts/router.md` | Intent classification and thread segmentation |\n| `prompts/synthesize.md` | Result synthesis (raw data → 2–3 sentence summary) |\n| `prompts/voice.md` | Narration style and pacing |\n| `prompts/urgency.md` | Urgency classification rules |\n\n### Project registry\n\n`config/registry.yaml` defines the projects aide-de-camp knows about. Each entry specifies:\n- `name` and `aliases` — how the router maps utterance fragments to projects\n- `cluster` and `namespace` — where to run kubectl-based fetch commands\n- `workflow_templates` — named multi-step workflows (e.g., \"deploy\", \"rollback\")\n\n---\n\n## Architecture\n\n### Key source files\n\n| File | Responsibility |\n|------|---------------|\n| `src/main.py` | FastAPI app; `/dispatch`, `/voice`, `/sse` endpoints |\n| `src/intent/router.py` | LLM classification and parallel thread dispatch |\n| `src/fetch/commands.py` | Fetch command matrix per intent type |\n| `src/fetch/orchestrator.py` | Parallel fetch execution |\n| `src/synthesize/strand.py` | LLM synthesis strand |\n| `src/session/store.py` | SQLite session store (aiosqlite) |\n| `src/sse/broadcaster.py` | SSE connection registry and event routing |\n| `src/canvas/index.html` | Canvas single-page app |\n| `src/realtime/session.py` | OpenAI Realtime API voice session |\n| `src/watcher/daemon.py` | Async work-item watcher — pushes completed results to canvas |\n| `src/agents/self_modification.py` | Self-improvement agent |\n| `src/environment/discovery.py` | Local and remote repo scanner |\n| `config/registry.yaml` | Explicit project registry |\n| `prompts/` | Hot-reload prompt files |\n\n### Runtime breakdown\n\n| Runtime | Used for |\n|---------|---------|\n| Realtime API | Voice model (persistent session, voice I/O) |\n| Configurable LLM backend | Intent router, synthesize strand |\n| Deterministic code | Fetch strand (kubectl/git execution), bead watcher |\n| Async worker fleet | UI-regen agent, self-modification agent, background analysis, task work items |\n\n### Data flow\n\n```\nPOST /dispatch\n  → intent router (LLM classify)\n  → N parallel: fetch strand → synthesize strand\n  → persist: topic + result in session.db\n  → broadcast SSEEvent(\"result_created\", surface_id)\n  → canvas calls GET /api/v1/sessions/{id}/topics\n  → cards rendered / updated in place\n```\n\n---\n\n## Requirements\n\n- Python 3.11+\n- `fastapi`, `uvicorn[standard]`, `httpx`, `websockets`, `pydantic`, `aiosqlite`\n- Dev: `pytest`, `pytest-asyncio`, `ruff`\n\n---\n\n## Versioning\n\nVersion is in `pyproject.toml` only.
